intent: Write a brief on 'pantry staples that actually save money' for a household trying to cut grocery spend. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) the One Stop Market shopping site — browse the grocery-gourmet-food category and collect at least 4 pantry staples (dry goods, oils/condiments, canned, and one spice/baking item). Record name, price, rating, and review count for each. (2) Reddit — browse /f/personalfinance AND /f/LifeProTips for posts about meal planning, bulk buys that pay off, or foods people consistently throw away. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, and comment count. The report must: (a) list the storefront staples with a 'cost per use' estimate where possible, (b) pull at least two practical rules from Reddit (e.g. what bulk buys backfire, which store brands are fine), (c) identify any high-rated storefront item the community would call a trap, (d) conclude with 5 items that belong in every budget pantry. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Shopping (grocery-gourmet-food) + Reddit (personalfinance + LifeProTips). Cost-per-use reasoning + community filtering.
